name: Lego Marker Compliance
summary: Validates that a generated miniApp artifact declares its Lego recipe via the canonical mchatai-web-components-used marker block, lists every required component, and uses a supported composition mode.

checks.name: Marker block present
checks.description: The HTML must contain a <script type="application/json" id="mchatai-web-components-used"> block. Without it the artifact is treated as a markerless monolith and rejected (or repaired via golden-assembly fallback if available).

checks.name: Marker declares the recipe matched by the matcher
checks.description: The marker's `recipe` field must equal the recipe id selected by the harness matcher (e.g. recipe.chess for a chess goal).

checks.name: All requiredComponents present in marker
checks.description: Every component listed in the recipe's requiredComponents must appear in the marker's components array. Optional components are allowed but not required.

checks.name: Composition mode is module-imports or single-file-inline
checks.description: Marker.mode must be one of module-imports (preferred) or single-file-inline (fallback). Other values fail the gate.

checks.name: Module-imports resolve to component paths OR inline blocks are properly fenced
checks.description: For module-imports mode, every component listed in marker must have a matching `import ... from "./web-components/<path>"` line. For single-file-inline mode, every component must be fenced by BEGIN/END comments with its component-id.
checks.rule: if marker.mode == 'module-imports': all(html.contains(component.path) for component in marker.components); else: all(html.contains('BEGIN mChatAI Web Component: ' + cid) for cid in marker.components)

checks.name: No internal architecture jargon in user-visible content
checks.description: User-visible content (titles, headings, instructions, prompts, placeholder text) must not contain phrases like 'Lego blocks', 'mini-app', 'web-component', 'mchatai-source', or other internal terminology.

checks.name: Game with terminal phase shows visible restart affordance
checks.description: For any game with game-over / you-win / level-complete / mission-failed states, the UI must show a tappable restart button (≥60×200px, labeled clearly) when the terminal phase is reached. Not a menu shortcut, not a tiny corner icon.

notes: These checks mirror what `frameworks/web-components/tests/check_component_usage.mjs` enforces today. As of LF.4 the validator distinguishes requiredComponents (must be in marker) from optionalComponents (allowed but not required) so games can omit nice-to-have layers like audio without failing the gate.

Phase LF.0a (2026-05-05): added Swift skeleton support for nested-dict contracts in component definitions so future content additions (typography vocab, sprite atlas metadata) don't silently break catalog decode. Confirmed via diagWebComponentMatch tunnel command.
